comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: the proposed three-year delay of the CWA Hazardous Substance Facility Response Plan (FRP) compliance deadline, from June 1, 2027, to June 1, 2030, as well as the proposed removal of certain language to reflect the current Administration’s policies. With respect to the proposed three-year delay, affected facilities have had ample notice since the rule was pro...

EPA-HQ-OLEM-2021-0585 To whom it may concern, I am an undergraduate environmental sciences student who has been studying the EPA and its requirements for facilities to mitigate environmental harm. The environment is a culmination of many earth systems on local, national, and global scales. These systems are complex, and sometimes interact in unexpected ways,...

I oppose this proposed regulation delaying the compliance date for the Facility Response Plan (FRP). The removal of this language suggests to Americans that climate change is not an important issue. Even more concerning, this regulation delay has potentially disastrous effects on the environment as hazardous materials continue to be contained as is if the EP...

I am opposed to the EPA proposing to delay FRP requirements. This proposal will increase the probability that some high-risk facilities will not yet be required to have detailed spill response plans, and there will be a longer window where a worst-case discharge could occur without the new safeguards. Even stated within the language of this rule, the EPA say...
